title: The Orange Pillow Fort
description: A modpack for my friends that I decided to publish. Focuses on expansive worldgen and combat, featuring create and the RPG series of mods
body:
The Orange Pillow Fort (previously known as The Orange Box when it was on fabric in 1.19.2) is a modpack that I have been continually working on for a year now for my friends. I decided to switch from Curseforge to Modrinth because a lot of the mods I want are over here now. 

The pack is 1.20.2 for quilt, and primarily focuses on world generation and building, but it has revamped combat using the Better Combat ecosystem of mods. 


A brief overview of  what this pack contains:
- Over 200 new overworld biomes
- Total end overhaul with 40+ new end biomes and new mobs
- The nether is now even hotter with 30+ new nether biomes and even more mobs
- New enemies to fight, including ghouls, dead kings, exploding jellyfish, new illager types, and even more terrifying things lurking in caves and beneath the waves
- So many structures I really don’t know how to count them, including dozens of new villages, 50+ dungeons, castles, and outposts
- Total ocean revamp, with larger landmasses and wider oceans full of hundreds of new fish, sharks, jellyfish
- Cappybaras, giraffes, elephants, butterflies, and other new passive land mobs
- New ways to move around with gliders, more boats, airships, and planes, in addition to wallrunning, sliding, and rolling
- Melee and ranged combat overhaul from the Better Combat/RPG series of mods, as well as magic
- Every melee weapon you could want, including legendary weapons with special abilities
- All the fun and industry the Create mod can provide, as well as the addons for farming, guns, and structures!
-Ambient sounds, wakes, cosmetic armor, better looking blocks, and more
- Over 100 new songs and music disks 
- Optimizations to worldgen, entity rendering and spawning, memory usage, load times, and more
- Most bedrock features
- And more fun features like backpacks, signposts, and a whole bunch of QOL features


Note: I tried to include everybody listed on Modrinth. But some people have changed their names, or may have too many people to list (mainly the music mods). I encourage you to look at the Modrinth pages for these creators as they may add new ways to support them! I did not include discord links, but about 80 percent of these mods have a discord you can join to get support, ideas, or talk with the creator and community. These are also listed on their Modrinth pages.
If a creator's name has a link on it, that's a link to a patreon, ko-fi, or some form of way to financially support the creator.

Seriously, if you see a feature you love, consider finding the creator and giving them some support. We couldn’t enjoy any of this without them.

I also suggest upping your ram a bit, the default amount can hardly handle the worldgen

*Here is a short trailer showcasing features*
